riskTerms.label: Runtime compatibility risk
riskTerms.description: The behavior change may alter how callers experience runtime coercion for unusual inputs.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: The npm test Evidence is observed, but the second fixture is not supported and the external checkout remains calibration-local.

riskTerms.label: Input coercion behavior risk
riskTerms.description: Changing coercion from concatenation to explicit String(value) may change pre-escape behavior if the coercion surface is misidentified.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: Anchor-level context is approximate and exact line ranges are not verified.

riskTerms.label: Symbol handling regression risk
riskTerms.description: Symbol input must stringify to Symbol(description) instead of throwing, without introducing unrelated behavior expansion.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: The Symbol assertion is observed in dogfood Evidence, but the check binding remains awaiting authoritative checkout.

riskTerms.label: Escaping correctness risk
riskTerms.description: Escaping of quote, apostrophe, ampersand, less-than, and greater-than characters must not drift while coercion changes.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: Existing escape-vocabulary anchors are previewed, but graph-delta review binding is not yet modeled.

riskTerms.label: Non-string input behavior drift
riskTerms.description: Existing stringification behavior for undefined, null, numbers, and objects must remain preserved.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: Existing test anchors are approximate and not supported compiler context.

riskTerms.label: Test coverage insufficiency risk
riskTerms.description: Observed checks may be insufficient if they do not cover the behavior anchors and forbidden escape-rule boundaries.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: External required-check binding is previewed but not authoritative, enforced, or supported.

riskTerms.label: Source authority boundary risk
riskTerms.description: Observed README/tests, local dogfood Evidence, and graph proposals may be mistaken for upstream maintainer approval or user acceptance.
riskTerms.notMitigatedReason: The preview records the boundary, but the second fixture has no promotion review packet or human approval.

anchorLinks.riskImpact: Approximate anchors make the risk vocabulary more precise than file-level context, but they do not prove exact affected lines or grant edit permission.

evidenceLinks.riskImpact: Observed Evidence can inform risk vocabulary, but it is not a supported required check, CI gate, promotion approval, or user acceptance.

unresolvedRiskGaps.reason: Risk vocabulary names risks but does not prove mitigation or equivalence.
The external checkout remains calibration-local, non-portable, and non-enforcing.
Anchor strings are approximate and exact line ranges are not verified.
Graph delta and graph update proposal bindings are not yet modeled as review-only output/reporting obligations.

allowedUse: Describe behavior-change-specific risks for the escape-html calibration fixture.
Connect risk terms to approximate anchors and observed Evidence for review.
Inform future graph-delta review binding and compliance-check design.
Keep risk terms bounded to this second fixture calibration pass.
forbiddenUse: Do not treat this vocabulary as final compiler risk policy.
Do not claim risk mitigation is complete.
Do not mark the second fixture as supported.
Do not generate a contract candidate from this preview.
Do not run external checks or create required checks from this preview.
Do not treat this preview as promotion approval, user acceptance, CI enforcement, branch protection, graph delta apply authority, or execution authority.

recommendedNextStep.summary: Decide whether graph-delta review binding should be the next v0.3 calibration scope.
recommendedNextStep.rationale: Risk vocabulary is previewed, and the remaining major calibration gap is binding graph delta and graph update proposal artifacts as review-only outputs without graph apply authority.

nonExecutionBoundary: This risk vocabulary preview names calibration risks only. It does not mark the second fixture as supported, wire it into compile-contract --dry-run, execute checks, modify files, compile a candidate, prove mitigation, create or approve a promotion packet, create CI enforcement, create GitHub required checks, configure branch protection, apply graph deltas, grant execution authority, automate user acceptance, or retire tree-native artifacts.
